key_insights.insight: Rewiring your brain requires awareness of existing patterns and triggers, often tracing back to understand the root cause of unwanted behaviors or moods.
key_insights.quote: In order to do that you have to recognize when your brain is doing a pattern that you want to rewire and quite often like it's already triggered so many things and like you're in a bad mood and you have no idea why

key_insights.insight: Anxiety can stem from inauthenticity and the fear of how one is perceived when not being true to oneself.
key_insights.quote: A lot of that anxiety as I started to sort of recognize it came from like not really being myself and like then feeling anxious about the way that you're being perceived

key_insights.insight: Acting can be a therapeutic outlet, allowing individuals to access and express difficult emotions in a safe and consequence-free environment.
key_insights.quote: I get to access all of that confusion and pain in my job and I get to like really feel it in like every fiber of my being but there's no like consequence and there's no like you're not really shouting at anyone or hurting someone or it's like it's all pretend. But like the emotion is is real.

key_insights.insight: Striving to be a good role model can sometimes feel inauthentic if it prioritizes being liked over genuine self-expression.
key_insights.quote: I kind of like wanted to like say the right thing and do the right thing and like act like I had everything figured out and like be a good role model. But, you know, which is all very good things to want. But it's like it wasn't like very authentic.

key_insights.insight: Meditation and spirituality can provide a sense of connection and support, aiding in combating feelings of unworthiness and navigating life's challenges.
key_insights.quote: I'm not alone like you know like there's something else um even on like the hardest days like there's something here that's like gonna be there like gonna you know take care of me

key_insights.insight: Romantic relationships can be a catalyst for personal growth, particularly when a partner demonstrates patience and helps to recognize self-sabotaging patterns.
key_insights.quote: Whenever I would start doing that and start going like oh they he like saw it within me and was like you see what you're doing like you're you're like trying to sabotage yourself again and like you can do that like I'm not going to stop you.

key_insights.insight: True freedom comes from questioning and redefining previously held beliefs about oneself and the world, opening up new possibilities for experiencing life.
key_insights.quote: Every single thing that I've defined myself as, it can all be different. And that's real freedom, I think.

key_insights.insight: Engaging in creative activities, regardless of skill level, can be incredibly fulfilling and beneficial for one's mental and emotional well-being.
key_insights.quote: Finding like what you create like very freely and like being able to do that as much as possibleis like an incredibly fulfilling thing and it doesn't have to be like painting or like thesevery sort of rudimentary like clear sort of artists industries

key_insights.insight: Even though difficult experiences can teach invaluable lessons and shape one's identity, it's important to acknowledge that no one deserves to suffer.
key_insights.quote: The things I experienced when I was a child, no person should ever experience at any point in their life. But it's taught me so much

actionable_advice.advice: Capture moments of uncertainty or anger to identify potential triggers.
actionable_advice.how_to: Reflect on why certain situations or social cues evoke strong emotional responses and trace them back to their origins.

actionable_advice.advice: Incorporate creative activities into your routine, regardless of your perceived artistic ability.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate a small portion of your week to exploring art, music, writing, or any activity that allows for self-expression.

actionable_advice.advice: When facing setbacks, avoid self-blame and practice self-compassion.
actionable_advice.how_to: Acknowledge the difficulty of the situation, treat yourself with kindness, and focus on what you can learn from the experience.

memorable_stories: Maisie's description of experiencing 'impending doom' as a child, contrasting with the carefree joy of other kids.
The account of being taken to the staff room by a teacher who recognized she wasn't eating breakfast
Story of how meeting her partner helped her to see and confront self-sabotaging behaviors in relationships.
Running out of ADHD meds whilst working in Paris and learning to not beat herself up about it
contrarian_claims: That focusing on being a 'good role model' can sometimes hinder authenticity.
The idea that 'wasted time' is a myth, as all experiences can contribute to creativity and well-being.
Success is not a destination but a happening every second
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Maisie Williams: The Painful Past Of A Game Of Thrones Star
